Atomic Media Group
Our Mission

Atomic Media Group covers AI and Robotics the intelligence and machines reshaping the world. We track the developments that matter most for executives, founders, and operators building in this era.

Every day our automated intelligence pipeline scans hundreds of sources, curates the signal, and delivers it in a format built for decision-makers who don't have time to waste.

🦾 AI & Robotics Intelligence Where machines think, robots walk, and the future of intelligence is decided.
Top Stories
AI
Pulling from the feed...
AI in the Real World
⚙️ In Production Deployed Now
⚖️
Harvey Enterprise Legal AI
Harvey is deployed inside Allen & Overy, PwC, and 100+ law firms drafting contracts, running due diligence, and surfacing case law at a fraction of associate hours. Billing rates haven't dropped. Volume has exploded.
⊟ LegalHarvey AI · Live
🏦
JPMorgan COiN Contract Intelligence
JPMorgan's COiN platform reviews 12,000 commercial credit agreements per year work that previously took 360,000 hours of lawyer time annually. Error rate is lower than human review.
⊟ FinanceJPMorgan · Live
🩺
Google DeepMind AlphaFold Drug Discovery
AlphaFold has predicted the structure of 200M+ proteins essentially the entire known universe of biology. Dozens of pharmaceutical companies are now using it to accelerate drug discovery pipelines by years.
⊟ HealthcareDeepMind · Live
🛒
Amazon AI-Powered Demand Forecasting
Amazon's ML forecasting stack now handles real-time inventory decisions across 350M+ SKUs globally predicting regional demand spikes before they happen and auto-repositioning stock across fulfillment centers.
⊟ RetailAmazon · Live
✈️
Airbus Generative Design for Aircraft Parts
Airbus uses generative AI to design aircraft partition brackets that are 45% lighter than human-designed counterparts. The A320 now flies with AI-optimized structural components across its entire fleet.
⊟ AerospaceAirbus · Live
🔭 On the Horizon 6–18 Months Out
🧑‍💼
Agentic AI in the Enterprise
Every major platform Salesforce, ServiceNow, SAP, Workday is shipping autonomous agent layers in 2025. The shift from AI as a tool to AI as a worker completing multi-step tasks is the defining enterprise transition of the decade.
⊟ EnterpriseMultiple · 2025–26
🎓
AI Tutors at Scale
Khan Academy's Khanmigo and emerging competitors are deploying personalized AI tutors to millions of students. Early data shows significant learning acceleration particularly for underserved students without access to private tutoring.
⊟ EducationKhan Academy + · 2025
🏗️
AI in Physical Infrastructure
Utilities and grid operators are deploying AI to manage load balancing, predict equipment failure, and optimize renewable energy dispatch in real time. The US grid's transition to AI-managed infrastructure is already underway.
⊟ InfrastructureMultiple · 2025–26
🎬
AI-Generated Film & Advertising
Major studios and ad agencies are piloting fully AI-generated video for B-roll, product ads, and localization. Sora, Runway, and Kling are already in agency workflows. Full AI feature films are 18–24 months from credibility.
⊟ MediaMultiple · 2025–26
🧬
AI-Designed Clinical Trials
Pharma companies are using AI to design smarter trials identifying optimal patient cohorts, predicting dropout risk, and simulating outcomes before a single dose is administered. Trial timelines are compressing from years to months.
⊟ PharmaMultiple · 2026
Robotics in the Real World
🏭 In Production Deployed Now
🤖
Figure 02 BMW Manufacturing
Figure AI's humanoid is operating autonomously on BMW's Spartanburg production floor handling parts, assembly support, and logistics tasks across full shifts without human intervention.
⊟ AutomotiveFigure AI · Live
📦
Agility Digit Amazon Warehouses
Digit robots are running 24/7 tote-handling operations across Amazon fulfillment centers. The pilot has cleared 1M+ cycles with error rates below 0.3% expansion to 10 facilities confirmed.
⊟ LogisticsAgility Robotics · Live
🏥
Moxi Hospital Delivery Networks
Diligent Robotics' Moxi is deployed in 50+ US hospitals autonomously delivering labs, meds, and supplies across floors freeing nurses from an average of 30 minutes of fetch tasks per shift.
⊟ HealthcareDiligent Robotics · Live
🚜
John Deere Autonomous Tractors
Over 500 fully autonomous 8R tractors are now operating on US farms plowing, planting, and spraying without operators. GPS + computer vision handles field navigation end-to-end.
⊟ AgricultureJohn Deere · Live
🍕
Flippy 2 Commercial Kitchens
Miso Robotics' Flippy 2 is frying, grilling, and plating at scale inside White Castle and Jack in the Box locations. Each unit handles the equivalent of 2 full-time workers during peak hours.
⊟ Food ServiceMiso Robotics · Live
🔭 On the Horizon 6–18 Months Out
⚡
Tesla Optimus Consumer Launch
Tesla has moved its consumer Optimus timeline to late 2026. Internal units are already handling parts sorting and battery assembly. Musk targets a $20K price point cheaper than a used car.
⊟ ConsumerTesla · Est. 2026
🦾
Boston Dynamics Atlas Enterprise Rollout
The fully electric Atlas is shipping to select enterprise partners in Q3. It can lift 25kg, climb stairs, and recover from falls autonomously. Construction and heavy industry are the first targets.
⊟ IndustrialBoston Dynamics · Q3 2025
🏠
1X NEO Home Assistant
1X Technologies, backed by OpenAI, is piloting NEO in real home environments. The bet: a soft, human-scale robot that handles chores, fetches items, and assists elderly users without feeling industrial.
⊟ Home1X Technologies · 2026
🚀
Apptronik Apollo NASA + Defense
Apollo is in active NASA testing for extraterrestrial surface operations think maintenance, construction, and EVA support on the Moon. Defense contracts are also in discussion for forward logistics.
⊟ AerospaceApptronik · 2026–27
🌏
China's Humanoid Surge
400+ robotics companies launched in China in 2025 alone. UBTECH, Fourier Intelligence, and Unitree are scaling fast with government backing. The race for global humanoid market share is accelerating.
⊟ GeopoliticalMultiple · Ongoing

By the Numbers
Global AI Investment
$329B
Deployed in AI ventures in 2024 up 62% YoY. 2025 on pace to exceed $500B.
↑ 62% year over year
Humanoids Deployed
12,400+
Humanoid robots in active commercial or industrial deployment globally as of Q2 2025.
↑ 8x from 2023 baseline
Models in Production
1,800+
Foundation models publicly available on Hugging Face. Up from 600 in early 2023.
↑ 3x in 24 months
NVIDIA Market Cap
$3.1T
NVIDIA briefly surpassed Apple as the world's most valuable company on GPU demand alone.
↑ #1 globally by market cap
Robotics Market Size
$218B
Global robotics market valuation in 2025. Projected to reach $500B+ by 2030 on humanoid acceleration.
↑ Projected 2x by 2030
Jobs Touched by AI
300M
Goldman Sachs estimate of roles globally exposed to AI automation equivalent to 18% of all work.
→ 18% of global workforce

🚀 The Frontier Humanity is leaving the planet. Every launch, mission, and breakthrough as it happens.

Space
Pulling from the feed...
The Frontier in the Real World

Deployed Now
No results
🔭 On the Horizon
6–18 Months Out
No results
Companies to Watch
🚀 Launch & Access
Live
No results
🛰️ Space Economy
No results
By the Numbers
GLOBAL LAUNCH ATTEMPTS
212
In 2023, humanity achieved a record 212 orbital launch attempts nearly doubling the pace of five years prior, driven by SpaceX, Rocket Lab, and China's growing launch cadence.
↑ Record high in 2023
COMMERCIAL SPACE ECONOMY
$630B
The global space economy surpassed $630B in 2023, with commercial activity now representing over 80% of total value led by satellite services, launch, and emerging in-orbit manufacturing.
↑ 8% year over year
STARLINK SATELLITES
6,000+
SpaceX's Starlink constellation surpassed 6,000 active satellites in 2024, delivering broadband internet to over 3 million subscribers across 100+ countries.
↑ Growing 40% annually
CREWED MISSIONS LAUNCHED
12
Twelve crewed orbital missions launched in 2023-2024, the most in any comparable period since the Space Shuttle era with commercial vehicles now carrying the majority.
↑ Most since Shuttle era
SPACE VENTURE FUNDING
$8.7B
Private space companies raised $8.7B in venture and private equity funding in 2023, with launch, in-space services, and satellite communications leading investment categories.
↑ 12% vs prior year
REUSABLE ROCKET LANDINGS
250+
SpaceX has successfully landed and reflown orbital-class booster rockets over 250 times, fundamentally changing the economics of space access with reuse factors exceeding 20x per vehicle.
↑ Redefining launch economics
Atomic's Predictions Predictions are reviewed and updated as new signals emerge.

🧬 Longevity The science of living longer is moving faster than most people know. Stay ahead of it.
Top Stories
Longevity
Pulling from the feed...
Longevity in the Real World
🔬 In Clinical Use
Deployed Now
No results
🧪 On the Horizon 6–18 Months Out
No results
Companies to Watch
🧬 Biotech & Research
Live
No results
💊 Therapeutics & Clinics
No results
By the Numbers
GLOBAL LONGEVITY MARKET
$25B
The longevity therapeutics and diagnostics market reached $25B in 2024, with projections to surpass $600B by 2025 as anti-aging interventions move from research into mainstream clinical use.
↑ Fastest growing health category
GLP-1 PRESCRIPTIONS (US)
9M+
Over 9 million Americans are now prescribed GLP-1 receptor agonists, with Ozempic and Wegovy reshaping metabolic health, obesity treatment, and now showing promise for cardiovascular and cognitive disease.
↑ 400% growth since 2021
LONGEVITY VC INVESTMENT
$5.2B
Venture capital invested $5.2B into longevity and aging-focused biotechs in 2023, with Altos Labs, Retro Biosciences, and NewLimit collectively raising over $1.5B from tech billionaires alone.
↑ 3x increase since 2020
HUMAN LIFESPAN EXTENSION TARGET
25 yrs
Leading longevity scientists project interventions currently in development could extend healthy human lifespan by 25 or more years within the next two decades moving from disease treatment to biological aging reversal.
↑ Escape velocity within reach
AI DRUG DISCOVERY SPEED
10x
AI-driven drug discovery platforms are identifying longevity candidates up to 10x faster than traditional methods, with companies like Insilico Medicine advancing molecules from target identification to clinical trials in under 18 months.
↑ Compressing decade-long timelines
CENTENARIAN POPULATION
722K
The global centenarian population exceeded 722,000 in 2024 and is projected to reach 3.7 million by 2050 driven by improvements in healthcare, nutrition, and the early effects of longevity-focused interventions.
↑ 5x growth projected by 2050
Atomic's Predictions Predictions are reviewed and updated as new signals emerge.

📖 Guides for Sales
📖
How to Use AI in Your Daily Sales Workflow
5 min read
→
📖
Writing Cold Outreach That Actually Gets Replies
4 min read
→
⚡ Prompts for Sales
Cold Outreach Email ⚡ Quick Win
"Write a cold outreach email to [Name] at [Company]. Their role is [Title]. We help [ICP] achieve [outcome]. Keep it under 100 words, no fluff, end wit..."
Follow-Up Sequence
"Write a 3-email follow-up sequence for a prospect who didnt respond to my first outreach. Company: [Company]. My value prop: [value prop]. Space them..."
Discovery Call Prep ⚡ Quick Win
"Prepare me for a discovery call with [Name], [Title] at [Company]. Research their business model, likely pain points for someone in their role, and gi..."
Deal Qualification
"Score this deal on MEDDIC: [paste deal notes]. Identify whats confirmed, whats missing, and what I need to do in the next meeting to advance or disq..."
Objection Handler ⚡ Quick Win
"Im getting this objection: [objection]. My product is [product] and the prospect is [context]. Give me 3 ways to handle this objection — one that ref..."
Proposal Email
"Write a proposal follow-up email after our meeting with [Company]. Key points discussed: [notes]. The proposal covers [scope] at [price]. Goal: create..."
Pipeline Review Prep
"I have a pipeline review tomorrow. Here are my deals: [paste list]. For each deal, identify the biggest risk, the most important next action, and whet..."
CRM Update Draft ⚡ Quick Win
"I just had a call with [Name] at [Company]. Heres what happened: [paste notes]. Write a clean CRM update including: what was discussed, next steps, c..."
Competitive Battle Card
"Build a quick battle card against [Competitor] for a deal at [Company]. Include: their key strengths, their key weaknesses, how we win against them, a..."
Forecast Commentary
"Write my weekly forecast commentary for my VP. Committed: $[X]. Best case: $[Y]. Pipeline: $[Z]. Key risks: [risks]. Key wins this week: [wins]. Tone ..."

The CS Playbook: Using AI to Prevent Churn Before It Happens

→
⚡ Prompts for Customer Success
QBR Preparation ⚡ Quick Win
"Prepare a QBR for [Customer Name] at [Company]. Contract value: [ARR]. Their stated goals when they bought: [goals]. Progress against goals this quart..."

Escalation Response ⚡ Quick Win

"Create a 90-day onboarding plan for [Customer] who just purchased [product]. Their goals: [goals]. Their team size: [size]. Technical resources availa..."
Churn Risk Assessment ⚡ Quick Win

Expansion Opportunity
"Identify expansion opportunities for [Customer]. Current products/tier: [what they have]. Their business: [description]. Known pain points: [list]. Us..."
Executive Sponsor Email

Writing PRDs That Engineers Actually Want to Build

→
⚡ Prompts for Product
PRD Writer ⚡ Quick Win

User Story Generator ⚡ Quick Win

Discovery Interview Guide ⚡ Quick Win

Launch Announcement ⚡ Quick Win

Post-Mortem Template

Acquisition Brief

Atomic Media Group
Our Mission

Atomic Media Group covers AI and Robotics the intelligence and machines reshaping the world. We track the developments that matter most for executives, founders, and operators building in this era.

Every day our automated intelligence pipeline scans hundreds of sources, curates the signal, and delivers it in a format built for decision-makers who don't have time to waste.

🦾 AI & Robotics Intelligence Where machines think, robots walk, and the future of intelligence is decided.
Top Stories
AI
Pulling from the feed...
AI in the Real World
⚙️ In Production Deployed Now
⚖️
Harvey Enterprise Legal AI
Harvey is deployed inside Allen & Overy, PwC, and 100+ law firms drafting contracts, running due diligence, and surfacing case law at a fraction of associate hours. Billing rates haven't dropped. Volume has exploded.
⊟ LegalHarvey AI · Live
🏦
JPMorgan COiN Contract Intelligence
JPMorgan's COiN platform reviews 12,000 commercial credit agreements per year work that previously took 360,000 hours of lawyer time annually. Error rate is lower than human review.
⊟ FinanceJPMorgan · Live
🩺
Google DeepMind AlphaFold Drug Discovery
AlphaFold has predicted the structure of 200M+ proteins essentially the entire known universe of biology. Dozens of pharmaceutical companies are now using it to accelerate drug discovery pipelines by years.
⊟ HealthcareDeepMind · Live
🛒
Amazon AI-Powered Demand Forecasting
Amazon's ML forecasting stack now handles real-time inventory decisions across 350M+ SKUs globally predicting regional demand spikes before they happen and auto-repositioning stock across fulfillment centers.
⊟ RetailAmazon · Live
✈️
Airbus Generative Design for Aircraft Parts
Airbus uses generative AI to design aircraft partition brackets that are 45% lighter than human-designed counterparts. The A320 now flies with AI-optimized structural components across its entire fleet.
⊟ AerospaceAirbus · Live
🔭 On the Horizon 6–18 Months Out
🧑‍💼
Agentic AI in the Enterprise
Every major platform Salesforce, ServiceNow, SAP, Workday is shipping autonomous agent layers in 2025. The shift from AI as a tool to AI as a worker completing multi-step tasks is the defining enterprise transition of the decade.
⊟ EnterpriseMultiple · 2025–26
🎓
AI Tutors at Scale
Khan Academy's Khanmigo and emerging competitors are deploying personalized AI tutors to millions of students. Early data shows significant learning acceleration particularly for underserved students without access to private tutoring.
⊟ EducationKhan Academy + · 2025
🏗️
AI in Physical Infrastructure
Utilities and grid operators are deploying AI to manage load balancing, predict equipment failure, and optimize renewable energy dispatch in real time. The US grid's transition to AI-managed infrastructure is already underway.
⊟ InfrastructureMultiple · 2025–26
🎬
AI-Generated Film & Advertising
Major studios and ad agencies are piloting fully AI-generated video for B-roll, product ads, and localization. Sora, Runway, and Kling are already in agency workflows. Full AI feature films are 18–24 months from credibility.
⊟ MediaMultiple · 2025–26
🧬
AI-Designed Clinical Trials
Pharma companies are using AI to design smarter trials identifying optimal patient cohorts, predicting dropout risk, and simulating outcomes before a single dose is administered. Trial timelines are compressing from years to months.
⊟ PharmaMultiple · 2026
Robotics in the Real World
🏭 In Production Deployed Now
🤖
Figure 02 BMW Manufacturing
Figure AI's humanoid is operating autonomously on BMW's Spartanburg production floor handling parts, assembly support, and logistics tasks across full shifts without human intervention.
⊟ AutomotiveFigure AI · Live
📦
Agility Digit Amazon Warehouses
Digit robots are running 24/7 tote-handling operations across Amazon fulfillment centers. The pilot has cleared 1M+ cycles with error rates below 0.3% expansion to 10 facilities confirmed.
⊟ LogisticsAgility Robotics · Live
🏥
Moxi Hospital Delivery Networks
Diligent Robotics' Moxi is deployed in 50+ US hospitals autonomously delivering labs, meds, and supplies across floors freeing nurses from an average of 30 minutes of fetch tasks per shift.
⊟ HealthcareDiligent Robotics · Live
🚜
John Deere Autonomous Tractors
Over 500 fully autonomous 8R tractors are now operating on US farms plowing, planting, and spraying without operators. GPS + computer vision handles field navigation end-to-end.
⊟ AgricultureJohn Deere · Live
🍕
Flippy 2 Commercial Kitchens
Miso Robotics' Flippy 2 is frying, grilling, and plating at scale inside White Castle and Jack in the Box locations. Each unit handles the equivalent of 2 full-time workers during peak hours.
⊟ Food ServiceMiso Robotics · Live
🔭 On the Horizon 6–18 Months Out
⚡
Tesla Optimus Consumer Launch
Tesla has moved its consumer Optimus timeline to late 2026. Internal units are already handling parts sorting and battery assembly. Musk targets a $20K price point cheaper than a used car.
⊟ ConsumerTesla · Est. 2026
🦾
Boston Dynamics Atlas Enterprise Rollout
The fully electric Atlas is shipping to select enterprise partners in Q3. It can lift 25kg, climb stairs, and recover from falls autonomously. Construction and heavy industry are the first targets.
⊟ IndustrialBoston Dynamics · Q3 2025
🏠
1X NEO Home Assistant
1X Technologies, backed by OpenAI, is piloting NEO in real home environments. The bet: a soft, human-scale robot that handles chores, fetches items, and assists elderly users without feeling industrial.
⊟ Home1X Technologies · 2026
🚀
Apptronik Apollo NASA + Defense
Apollo is in active NASA testing for extraterrestrial surface operations think maintenance, construction, and EVA support on the Moon. Defense contracts are also in discussion for forward logistics.
⊟ AerospaceApptronik · 2026–27
🌏
China's Humanoid Surge
400+ robotics companies launched in China in 2025 alone. UBTECH, Fourier Intelligence, and Unitree are scaling fast with government backing. The race for global humanoid market share is accelerating.
⊟ GeopoliticalMultiple · Ongoing

By the Numbers
Global AI Investment
$329B
Deployed in AI ventures in 2024 up 62% YoY. 2025 on pace to exceed $500B.
↑ 62% year over year
Humanoids Deployed
12,400+
Humanoid robots in active commercial or industrial deployment globally as of Q2 2025.
↑ 8x from 2023 baseline
Models in Production
1,800+
Foundation models publicly available on Hugging Face. Up from 600 in early 2023.
↑ 3x in 24 months
NVIDIA Market Cap
$3.1T
NVIDIA briefly surpassed Apple as the world's most valuable company on GPU demand alone.
↑ #1 globally by market cap
Robotics Market Size
$218B
Global robotics market valuation in 2025. Projected to reach $500B+ by 2030 on humanoid acceleration.
↑ Projected 2x by 2030
Jobs Touched by AI
300M
Goldman Sachs estimate of roles globally exposed to AI automation equivalent to 18% of all work.
→ 18% of global workforce

Space
Pulling from the feed...

Deployed Now

6–18 Months Out
No results
Companies to Watch
🚀 Launch & Access
Live
No results
🛰️ Space Economy
No results
By the Numbers
GLOBAL LAUNCH ATTEMPTS
212
In 2023, humanity achieved a record 212 orbital launch attempts nearly doubling the pace of five years prior, driven by SpaceX, Rocket Lab, and China's growing launch cadence.
↑ Record high in 2023
COMMERCIAL SPACE ECONOMY
$630B
The global space economy surpassed $630B in 2023, with commercial activity now representing over 80% of total value led by satellite services, launch, and emerging in-orbit manufacturing.
↑ 8% year over year
STARLINK SATELLITES
6,000+
SpaceX's Starlink constellation surpassed 6,000 active satellites in 2024, delivering broadband internet to over 3 million subscribers across 100+ countries.
↑ Growing 40% annually
CREWED MISSIONS LAUNCHED
12
Twelve crewed orbital missions launched in 2023-2024, the most in any comparable period since the Space Shuttle era with commercial vehicles now carrying the majority.
↑ Most since Shuttle era
SPACE VENTURE FUNDING
$8.7B
Private space companies raised $8.7B in venture and private equity funding in 2023, with launch, in-space services, and satellite communications leading investment categories.
↑ 12% vs prior year
REUSABLE ROCKET LANDINGS
250+
SpaceX has successfully landed and reflown orbital-class booster rockets over 250 times, fundamentally changing the economics of space access with reuse factors exceeding 20x per vehicle.
↑ Redefining launch economics

🧬 Longevity The science of living longer is moving faster than most people know. Stay ahead of it.
Top Stories
Longevity
Pulling from the feed...
Longevity in the Real World
🔬 In Clinical Use
Deployed Now
No results
🧪 On the Horizon 6–18 Months Out
No results
Companies to Watch
🧬 Biotech & Research
Live
No results
💊 Therapeutics & Clinics
No results
By the Numbers
GLOBAL LONGEVITY MARKET
$25B
The longevity therapeutics and diagnostics market reached $25B in 2024, with projections to surpass $600B by 2025 as anti-aging interventions move from research into mainstream clinical use.
↑ Fastest growing health category
GLP-1 PRESCRIPTIONS (US)
9M+
Over 9 million Americans are now prescribed GLP-1 receptor agonists, with Ozempic and Wegovy reshaping metabolic health, obesity treatment, and now showing promise for cardiovascular and cognitive disease.
↑ 400% growth since 2021
LONGEVITY VC INVESTMENT
$5.2B
Venture capital invested $5.2B into longevity and aging-focused biotechs in 2023, with Altos Labs, Retro Biosciences, and NewLimit collectively raising over $1.5B from tech billionaires alone.
↑ 3x increase since 2020
HUMAN LIFESPAN EXTENSION TARGET
25 yrs
Leading longevity scientists project interventions currently in development could extend healthy human lifespan by 25 or more years within the next two decades moving from disease treatment to biological aging reversal.
↑ Escape velocity within reach
AI DRUG DISCOVERY SPEED
10x
AI-driven drug discovery platforms are identifying longevity candidates up to 10x faster than traditional methods, with companies like Insilico Medicine advancing molecules from target identification to clinical trials in under 18 months.
↑ Compressing decade-long timelines
CENTENARIAN POPULATION
722K
The global centenarian population exceeded 722,000 in 2024 and is projected to reach 3.7 million by 2050 driven by improvements in healthcare, nutrition, and the early effects of longevity-focused interventions.
↑ 5x growth projected by 2050
Atomic's Predictions Predictions are reviewed and updated as new signals emerge.

✍️ Writing
8 tools
🧠
Claude
Writing / Thinking
Best-in-class for long-form writing, nuanced editing, strategic memos, and executive communication. Handles complex context better than any other model.
EmailsMemosEditing
Try Claude →
💬
ChatGPT
Writing / General
The most widely used AI writing assistant. Strong for drafting, brainstorming, and first-pass content across any format or tone.
DraftingBrainstormContent
Try ChatGPT →
📝
Notion AI
Writing / Docs
AI writing built directly into your workspace. Summarize meeting notes, draft documents, and rewrite content without switching tools.
DocsSummariesNotes
Try Notion AI →
✅
Grammarly
Writing / Editing
Real-time writing suggestions across tone, clarity, and correctness. Works everywhere you write — email, docs, Slack, browser.
EditingToneClarity
Try Grammarly →
🚀
Jasper
Writing / Marketing
AI writing platform built for marketing teams. Templates for ads, blogs, landing pages, and social. Trained on high-converting copy.
MarketingAdsCopy
Try Jasper →
🎨
Gamma
Writing / Presentations
AI-generated presentations, documents, and websites from a prompt. No design skills needed. Output looks polished in minutes.
DecksDocsDesign
Try Gamma →
✒️
Hemingway
Writing / Clarity
Paste any text and it highlights sentences that are too complex, passive voice, and weak adverbs. Forces cleaner, more direct writing instantly.
ClarityEditingSimplicity
Try Hemingway →
📄
Lex
Writing / Long-form
AI writing tool built for long-form content. Ask for suggestions mid-draft without losing your train of thought. Designed for writers who think in paragraphs.
Long-formDraftingEssays
Try Lex →
🎵
Wordtune
Writing / Rewriting
AI writing companion that rewrites sentences while preserving your voice and intent. Offers multiple tone options: casual, formal, shorter, longer — all in one click.
RewritingToneVoice
Try Wordtune →
⚡ Prompts for Writing
Rewrite for Clarity
"Rewrite this text to be clearer, shorter, and more direct. Remove hedging, passive voice, and filler. Keep it under 75 words. Here is the original: [paste text]"
Executive Email
"Write a professional email to [Name/Title] about [topic]. Be concise, lead with what I need, and keep it under 100 words. Tone: [formal/conversational]."
Turn Notes into Doc
"Turn these rough notes into a polished, well-structured document with clear sections and professional language: [paste notes]"

8 tools
🔎
Perplexity
Research / Search
AI-powered search that returns answers with cited sources. Best for real-time research, market intelligence, and competitive analysis.
SearchCitationsReal-time

Elicit
Research / Analysis
AI research assistant that finds, summarizes, and synthesizes academic papers and reports. Ideal for deep due diligence and evidence-based decisions.

Consensus
Research / Science
Search the scientific literature using natural language. Ask questions, get answers backed by peer-reviewed research with direct citations.
ScienceEvidenceCitations

🌐
You.com
Research / Search
AI search engine with real-time web access and source attribution. Good for current events, product research, and fast fact-checking.
WebReal-timeSearch
Try You.com →
📓
NotebookLM
Research / Synthesis

DocumentsSynthesisQ&A
Try NotebookLM →
💎
Gemini Deep Research
Research / Deep Dive
Google Gemini's Deep Research mode autonomously browses the web, synthesizes findings, and produces structured research reports on any topic in minutes.
Deep ResearchWebReports
Try Gemini →
🔭
Tavily
Research / API
AI-optimized search API built for research workflows. Returns clean, structured results that AI agents and researchers can immediately use without extra scraping.
Search APIStructuredAgents

🔵
Copilot Search
Research / Integrated
Microsoft Copilot with Bing integration. Real-time web search combined with GPT-4 reasoning. Great for quick research, summarizing web pages, and fact-checking.
SearchReal-timeMicrosoft
Try Copilot →
🏛️
Scopus AI
Research / Academic
AI-enhanced academic database with 90M+ research records. Identify research trends, compare sources, and surface the most relevant literature for any professional question.
AcademicLiteratureTrends
Try Scopus →
⚡ Prompts for Research
Competitive Analysis
"Analyze [Company] vs [Company]. Compare their product, pricing, positioning, and target customer. What are the top three differentiators and who wins in an enterprise deal?"
Summarize for Exec
"Summarize this research into three bullet points an executive can act on. Focus on implications, not just facts. Here is the source: [paste content]"
Market Overview
"Give me a concise market overview of [industry/topic]. Include market size, key players, major trends, and the biggest open question in the space right now."

⚡ Prompts for Sales
Cold Outreach Email
"Write a personalized cold outreach email to [Name], [Title] at [Company]. They recently [trigger]. We help [ICP] achieve [outcome]. Under 100 words. End with a low-friction ask."
Deal Strategy
"I have a deal with [Company]. Budget: [X]. Decision maker: [Title]. Competition: [Competitors]. Stage: [Stage]. Key risk: [Risk]. What are the three most important things I should do this week?"
Follow-Up After Meeting
"Write a follow-up email after a sales meeting with [Company]. Key points discussed: [notes]. Next step agreed: [next step]. Keep it brief, warm, and action-oriented."

⚡ Prompts for Marketing
LinkedIn Post
"Turn this insight into a compelling LinkedIn post: [insight]. Punchy, short paragraphs, no hashtag spam, no generic motivation. Audience: executives and operators. End with a question."
Campaign Brief
"Write a one-page campaign brief for [campaign name]. Include: objective, target audience, key message, channels, success metrics, and timeline. Keep it tight enough to share with a team."
Content Repurposing
"Take this long-form content and repurpose it into: one LinkedIn post, one tweet thread opener, and one email subject line. Keep the core insight but adapt the format. Content: [paste]"

⚡ Prompts for Finance
Financial Summary
"Summarize this financial data for an executive audience. Highlight three key trends, one concern, and one action recommendation. Under 150 words. Data: [paste data]"
Budget Justification
"Write a business case to justify [budget request] for [project/tool]. Include: problem it solves, expected ROI, risks of not doing it, and a recommended timeline."
Variance Explanation
"We are [over/under] budget by [amount] in [category]. Write a clear explanation for leadership that includes the root cause, impact, and proposed corrective action."

⚡ Prompts for HR
Job Description
"Write a compelling job description for a [role] at [company type]. Ideal candidate: [qualities]. Avoid clichés. Be direct about what the role involves day to day."
Performance Review
"Write a balanced performance review for an employee who [achievements] but also [areas to improve]. Tone: constructive and forward-looking. Keep it under 200 words."
Interview Questions
"Generate 8 behavioral interview questions for a [role] candidate. Include questions that assess [key competency 1], [key competency 2], and culture fit. Avoid generic questions."

⚡ Prompts for Operations
Process Improvement
"Here is our current process for [task]: [describe]. Identify the top three inefficiencies, suggest what could be automated with AI today, and estimate time saved per week."
SOP Draft
"Write a clear, step-by-step SOP for [process/task]. Include: purpose, who is responsible, steps in order, decision points, and what to do when things go wrong."
Meeting Agenda
"Create a structured agenda for a [type] meeting with [team/stakeholders]. Goal: [outcome]. Duration: [time]. Include time allocations and a clear decision or action for each item."

⚡ Prompts for Legal
Contract Risk Review
"Review this contract and identify: (1) non-standard or high-risk clauses, (2) missing standard protections, (3) ambiguous language. Summarize in plain English."
NDA Draft
"Draft an NDA between [Company A] and [Company B] for [purpose]. Mutual confidentiality, [X]-year term, standard exclusions, governing law for [State]. Concise and business-friendly."
Plain English Summary
"Summarize this legal document in plain English for a non-lawyer. Focus on: what we are agreeing to, key obligations, default consequences, and exit terms."

⚡ Prompts for Executives
Board Update Memo
"Write a board update memo: key wins, misses and learnings, top 3 next-quarter priorities, and one decision needed from the board. Executive tone, one page max."
Difficult Leadership Communication
"Help me communicate this difficult decision to my team: [decision]. Clear, direct, explains the why, acknowledges impact, builds confidence forward. No corporate speak."
Strategic Decision Framework
"Strategic decision: [decision]. Key factors: [factors]. Risks: [risks]. Upside: [upside]. Act as my strategic advisor and give an honest recommendation with clear reasoning."

⚡ Prompts for Customer Success
QBR Preparation
"Prepare a QBR for [Customer]. Goals: [goals]. Progress: [progress]. Challenges: [challenges]. Priorities: [priorities]. Format as executive narrative with slide outline."
Renewal Outreach Email
"Write a renewal outreach to [Customer] at [Company]. Renews in [X] days. Value delivered: [value]. Known risk: [risk]. Goal is a meeting. Warm, specific, under 150 words."
Escalation Response
"Customer escalated. Issue: [issue]. Sentiment: [sentiment]. Context: [context]. Acknowledge the problem, show ownership, outline next steps. No deflection."

⚡ Prompts for Product
PRD Writer
"Write a PRD for [feature]. Problem: [problem]. Target user: [user]. Success metrics: [metrics]. Out of scope: [out of scope]. Include Overview, Goals, Requirements, Open Questions."
Feature Prioritization
"Feature requests: [list]. Quarter priorities: [priorities]. Engineering capacity: [capacity]. Prioritize by impact vs effort and recommend what to build next."
User Story Generator
"Write 5 user stories for [feature] as: As a [user], I want [action] so that [benefit]. Include acceptance criteria. Target user: [user]. Core use case: [use case]."

⚡ Prompts for Strategy
Competitive Analysis
"Full competitive analysis on [Competitor]: positioning, target customer, differentiators, pricing, weaknesses, investment areas. Where are the gaps we could exploit?"
Go-to-Market Plan
"Build a GTM strategy for [product]. ICP: [customer]. Problem solved: [problem]. Competition: [competitors]. Our advantage: [advantage]. Budget: [budget]. Timeline: [timeline]."
Devil's Advocate
"Strategic move: [decision]. Play devil's advocate. Strongest arguments against it? Assumptions that could be wrong? What would have to be true for this to fail?"
